title: Check-in modal missing service options
root_cause: openRoomCheckin() <select id="rc-svc"> only listed PT/OT/Eval/Re-eval/Speech. Acupuncture, Cupping, Massage, E-stim were absent.
fix: Added the missing options to match the Start Service modal.
ui_test: npx playwright test --grep "check-in modal service dropdown includes acupuncture"

title: Checkout and Treatment modals missing initial service
root_cause: openCheckout() and openTreatments() only fetched VisitTreatment rows. The initial service_type set at ServiceStart lived on the Visit record and was never shown.
fix: Removed special-case display logic. doRoomCheckin() and doAssignSvc() now POST /visits/{vid}/treatments/add immediately after service/start, persisting the initial service as a real VisitTreatment.
ui_test: npx playwright test --grep "treatment modality|treatment records tab"

title: Sign sheet PDF — treatments grouped on one row instead of separate lines
root_cause: generate_sign_sheet() built one row per visit, joining all treatment modalities with ', '.
fix: Expanded to one row per treatment. Falls back to one row with service_type when no VisitTreatment records exist.

title: Sign sheet PDF — missing duration on each row
root_cause: Service column in generate_sign_sheet() showed modality name only, no duration.
fix: Format Service cell as 'PT (30m)' or 'Acupuncture (20m)'. Widened Service column from 24mm to 30mm.

title: Treatment tab A/PT/CP/TN columns missing duration
root_cause: _col_display() in list_visits_with_treatments() returned only the therapist name; duration_minutes was not included in the cell string.
fix: _col_display() now returns 'Name / Xm'. Added GET /visit-records endpoint. Frontend loadTreatmentRecords() rewritten to one-row-per-visit with A/PT/CP/TN columns.

title: Daily summary total payment excludes copay
root_cause: get_daily_summary() summed only payment_amount; copay_collected was a separate field and was not included.
fix: payment_total = sum(copay_collected + payment_amount) for each checked-out visit.
api_test: cd backend && python -m pytest tests/test_prd004_features.py -k 'daily_summary or copay_total' -q

title: Staff hours use wall-clock time instead of treatment durations
root_cause: get_staff_hours() summed service_end_time - service_start_time (wall-clock). Showed '3m' when actual treatment durations were 30m + 50m.
fix: Rewrote to sum VisitTreatment.duration_minutes per therapist_id. Falls back to wall-clock only when no treatment records exist.
ui_test: npx playwright test --grep "report staff hours reflect treatment duration"

title: Check-in and Start Service modals missing duration field
root_cause: openRoomCheckin() and openAssignSvc() had no input for treatment duration. Duration was not captured at the point of service start.
fix: Added <input id="rc-dur"> (default 30) to check-in modal and <input id="as-dur"> (default 30) to Start Service modal. Value passed to auto-created VisitTreatment.

title: Initial service never persisted as VisitTreatment
root_cause: doRoomCheckin() and doAssignSvc() only called POST /portal/service/start. The service_type lived on the Visit model only, making it invisible in treatment modal, checkout summary, sign-sheet PDF, and treatment records tab.
fix: Both functions now POST /visits/{vid}/treatments/add immediately after service/start. Removed all 'prepend initial service' display hacks from openTreatments() and openCheckout().
ui_test: npx playwright test --grep "treatment modality|treatment records tab"

title: Treatment column blank when no therapist assigned even if duration exists
root_cause: _col_display() returned '' whenever therapist_id was null, even when duration_minutes > 0. Duration was accumulated in totals but silently dropped from the A/PT/CP/TN cell.
fix: _col_display() now returns 'Xm' (e.g. '45m') when duration exists but no therapist is assigned.

title: E2E Playwright tests failing after treatment tab redesign
root_cause: Three tests were written against the old per-treatment-row design. After redesign to per-visit grouped layout with A/PT/CP/TN columns: (1) expected duration '1h30m' but initial 30m + added 90m = '2h'; (2) expected old column headers; (3) expected 'Massage' as plain text (now in TN column) and '45m' was blank due to BUG-10.
fix: Updated assertions: duration '1h30m' → '2h'; headers updated to ['#','Date','Patient','主诊医生','A','PT','CP','TN','Room','Duration','Note']; removed Massage text assertion; '45m' now visible after BUG-10 fix.

title: Room board not grouped by floor despite floor data existing
root_cause: Database rooms table has floor field (16F, 18F) but frontend displays flat list. Paper room chart clearly separates 16F (1601-1609) from 18F (1801-1810). Data exists, just not grouped in UI.
fix: TBD - See GAP-UI-01 for implementation

title: Insurance copay not pre-filled at checkout
root_cause: Checkout modal did not pre-fill the Co-Pay amount. Insurance copay_amount existed in DB but was not fetched or displayed, so staff had to manually look it up and type it.
fix: openCheckout() now fetches the patient's primary insurance policy and pre-fills the Co-Pay amount field (`#co-cc`). It no longer auto-marks the separate CC-collected checkbox.
ui_test: npx playwright test --grep "checkout modal pre-fills copay amount from patient insurance"

title: Secondary insurance not displayed in patient modal
root_cause: Patient modal only shows primary insurance (priority=1). Patients with secondary insurance (priority=2) have it in database but UI doesn't display. Paper ledger has dedicated Primary/Secondary sections.
fix: TBD - See GAP-INS-09 for implementation

title: Patient creation does not require DOB or phone
root_cause: createPatient() only validated first/last name. date_of_birth and phone were optional in both the frontend form and PatientCreate Pydantic schema. Patients could be created with no contact info or DOB, leaving sign sheets and records incomplete.
fix: Moved DOB and phone to required fields in PatientCreate schema. Frontend createPatient() now validates both before submitting. Form layout updated to show DOB and phone on the same row. Tests and seed data updated with date_of_birth and phone.
api_test: cd backend && python -m pytest tests/test_prd004_features.py tests/test_treatments.py -q
ui_test: npx playwright test --grep "patient creation requires date of birth and phone"

title: Sign sheet PDF used CC column for dollar amount instead of collected checkbox semantics
root_cause: The PDF renderer treated the paper form's `CC` column as a dollar amount field. That mixed up two separate concepts: `Co-Pay` is the amount, while `CC` is the collected checkbox.
fix: The PDF header now shows the Co-Pay amount, preferring the saved visit copay when available. The table's `CC` column now renders only a collected checkmark based on the saved payment state.
ui_test: npx playwright test --grep "sign sheet PDF shows Co-Pay amount from insurance for unchecked-out visits"

title: AI/OCR ghost fields incorrectly recorded as real treatment and room-sheet fields
root_cause: Form-analysis documentation included AI/OCR ghost fields and abbreviations that were not actually present or confirmed on the source forms. The misreads were treatment-record fields `OUT`, `WW`, `CAST/GAS`, `Sig` and room-sheet status codes `BA`, `RA`, `MA`, `NA`. These then propagated into the unresolved-assumptions question list as if they were real business fields.
fix: Removed the OCR-derived ghost fields `OUT`, `WW`, `CAST/GAS`, `Sig`, `BA`, `RA`, `MA`, and `NA` from the active gap-analysis tables and business-owner question list. Replaced them with explicit correction notes so the repo no longer treats those mistaken labels as real domain terms.

title: Unauthenticated debug endpoints exposed in production
root_cause: /debug-db, /prototype/debug/httpx-test, and /prototype/debug/room-board-steps had no auth guard. /debug-db leaked DATABASE_URL prefix and full tracebacks. /debug/httpx-test was an SSRF vector. /debug/room-board-steps ran live DB queries.
fix: Removed all three endpoints from main.py and db_routes.py.

title: SECRET_KEY has hardcoded insecure fallback allowing JWT forgery
root_cause: jwt_utils.py used os.getenv('SECRET_KEY', 'dev-secret-key-change-in-production'). If env var was not set in deployment, any attacker who read the source could forge admin JWT tokens for any clinic_id.
fix: Removed the default. App now raises RuntimeError at startup if SECRET_KEY is absent. Added conftest.py to set the env var for tests before any app module imports.

title: Admin password printed in plaintext to server logs at first boot
root_cause: _seed_test_clinic() in main.py printed 'admin: admin@test.clinicos.local / test1234' to stdout. If logs are aggregated (Supabase, CloudWatch, Vercel), the password is visible to anyone with log access.
fix: Removed the password from the print statement. Now logs only the username and clinic_id.

title: CORS wildcard origin with allow_credentials=True
root_cause: CORSMiddleware was configured with allow_origins=['*'] and allow_credentials=True. This combination violates the CORS spec — browsers block credentials with wildcard origins — and would allow any origin to receive credentials if the wildcard check were ever relaxed.
fix: Replaced ['*'] with an explicit allowlist: localhost:8000, 127.0.0.1:8000, clinicos-psi.vercel.app. Additional origins can be added via CORS_ALLOWED_ORIGINS env var.

title: Checkout modal conflated save-vs-checkout and pre-checkout PDFs omitted saved copay
root_cause: The checkout flow only had one backend action: checkout. That forced the UI's PDF/save action to also check the patient out. The UI and PDF also conflated the paper form's `CC` checkbox with the numeric Co-Pay amount, so saved payment data and downloaded PDFs used inconsistent semantics.
fix: Split the flow into two actions: POST /portal/payment/save saves payment fields without changing visit status, while POST /portal/checkout now requires patient_signed=true before checkout. The modal now exposes separate Save Payment + PDF and Check Out buttons, disables checkout until Patient Signed is checked, preserves explicit 0.00 values, uses a dedicated `CC collected` checkbox separate from the Co-Pay amount field, resolves patient_id from an authoritative visit lookup when needed, and renders PDFs with Co-Pay as the amount plus CC as a collected checkbox marker.
api_test: cd backend && SECRET_KEY=test-secret python3 -m pytest tests/test_prd004_features.py -q
ui_test: SECRET_KEY=test-secret npx playwright test frontend/tests/e2e/local-smoke.spec.ts --grep "signed checkout without payment info|checkout collects copay CC, WD verified, and patient signed|checkout modal has copay CC, WD, and signed fields|checkout modal PDF action saves payment info before downloading|checkout requires patient signature checkbox|checkout does not trigger PDF download"
